articles.title: NASA’s Psyche Mission Aces Mars Flyby, Targets Metal-Rich Asteroid
articles.summary: NASA’s Psyche spacecraft completed its close approach of Mars on May 15, coming within 2,864 miles (4,609 kilometers) of the planet’s surface. This flyby used a gravity assist from Mars to provide a critical boost in speed and to adjust the spacecraft’s orbital plane without using any onboard propellant, sending it on its way toward […]

articles.title: Moon-Venus Conjunction
articles.summary: The Moon and Venus, center, are seen in conjunction above the Washington Monument, Monday, May 18, 2026, as viewed from the Mary W. Jackson NASA Headquarters Building in Washington. The Moon and Venus look close together because they line up from our point of view on Earth. In reality, they are separated by millions of […]

articles.title: Johnson’s Cindy Evans Prepares Artemis Teams for Lunar Science
articles.summary: NASA’s Artemis II crew had many technical and operational responsibilities during their historic mission to the Moon, but they also served an important role as scientific ambassadors to Earth’s nearest neighbor. On their 10-day journey, the crew flew by the far side of the Moon, analyzing and photographing geologic features such as impact craters and […]

articles.title: Startup challenging satellite industry’s multi-orbit playbook
articles.summary:
Contrivian argues that stitching together GEO, MEO and LEO satellites creates networking problems, and that LEO constellations alone can provide both resilience and speed
The post Startup challenging satellite industry’s multi-orbit playbook appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: Portal Space taps Quindar for ground mission support of its maneuvering spacecraft
articles.summary:
Portal is preparing to launch maneuverable spacecraft aimed at the military market

articles.title: NASA’s New Shock Detectives Project Invites Volunteers to Help Study Solar Wind
articles.summary: NASA’s Magnetospheric Multiscale (MMS) mission has collected more than ten years of data from this zone – more than scientists can analyze alone. As Shock Detectives, you’ll help sort the chaotic from peaceful regions of the data, giving researchers a crucial set of clues.

articles.title: Vast Space seeks to diversify by building satellites as well as space stations
articles.summary: "Every single successful space company is diversified in its products."

articles.title: Vast announces line of high-power satellite buses
articles.summary:
Commercial space station developer Vast is moving into satellite manufacturing with a line of high-power satellite buses.

articles.title: Lynk and Anterix get FCC nod to test satellite D2D for private utility networks
articles.summary:
Lynk Global has secured regulatory approval to test how its direct-to-device satellites could extend private utility networks using terrestrial spectrum from Anterix in the United States.
The post Lynk and Anterix get FCC nod to test satellite D2D for private utility networks appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: Anderson confirmed as NASA deputy administrator
articles.summary:
The U.S. Senate confirmed Matt Anderson on May 18 as NASA’s deputy administrator, the second-in-command of the space agency.
The post Anderson confirmed as NASA deputy administrator appeared first on SpaceNews.

articles.title: Smile launch highlights
articles.summary: ESA’s Smile satellite launched aboard a Vega-C rocket from Europe’s Spaceport in French Guiana. The rocket lifted off on at 04:52 BST / 05:52 CEST (00:52 local time) on 19 May 2026.

articles.title: ESA-China SMILE mission lifts off to deliver first global images of Earth’s magnetosphere
articles.summary:
The SMILE mission developed jointly by the European Space Agency and China has reached orbit after more than a decade of preparations and cooperation.
The post ESA-China SMILE mission lifts off to deliver first global images of Earth’s magnetosphere appeared first on SpaceNews.
